question_title: Will US spot Ethereum ETFs record net inflows for the trading week ending Friday, June 12, 2026?
summary: US spot Ethereum ETFs have been in a severe bearish outflow trend, with 15 consecutive outflow days as of June 1, 2026, and May 2026 seeing -$541M in total outflows. Only 1 of 4 May weeks was net positive. The target week (June 8-12) is still 4-5 trading days away, but the accelerating outflow trend (-$43 to -$60M/day recently) makes a positive weekly sum unlikely. However, the concentrated nature of outflows (77% from BlackRock ETHA) and extended streak length provide some contrarian reversion potential. I estimate an 18% probability of net positive weekly flows, reflecting the strong bearish momentum tempered by the possibility that the streak exhausts itself or a catalyst triggers reversal.

factors.description: 15 consecutive trading days of net outflows (May 11 - June 1, 2026), the longest streak in 2026. Strong bearish momentum that could extend through the target week.
Average daily ETH ETF outflow in May was -$27.1M/day, with recent weeks averaging -$43 to -$60M/day. A 5-day positive week requires overcoming ~$135-300M in expected outflows.
Only 5 of 20 trading days in May 2026 were positive (25%), showing isolated inflow days are rare and insufficient to offset outflow days.
ETH price at ~$1,987, down 59.8% from ATH. Depressed prices reduce institutional appetite and attract more outflows from loss-cutting.
BlackRock ETHA drove 77% of outflows, suggesting concentrated institutional repositioning that may run its course, potentially allowing reversal.
Extended outflow streaks historically revert - contrarian signal noted by analysts. Week of May 1-8 saw +$171.5M, proving positive weeks are possible even in bearish months.
Both BTC and ETH ETFs seeing severe outflows suggests macro-driven selling. If macro conditions improve (rate cut signals, risk-on shift), both could reverse together.

sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will US spot Ethereum ETFs record net inflows for the trading week ending Friday, June 12, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43748)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if the sum of daily "Total" net flows across all US spot Ethereum ETFs for the five US trading days of Monday June 8, 2026 through Friday June 12, 2026 (inclusive) is strictly greater than zero US dollars, as reported on Farside Investors' Ethereum ETF Flow page (https://farside.co.uk/eth/). Otherwise it resolves NO. If Farside Investors is unavailable or has not updated by June 19, 2026, the SoSoValue Ethereum spot ETF dashboard (https://sosovalue.com/assets/etf/us-eth-spot) will be used as a fallback, summing the "Daily Total Net Inflow (USD)" values for those same five trading days.
fine_print: If one or more of those dates is an unscheduled US market closure, only the trading days that actually occurred in that calendar week (Mon June 8 – Fri June 12, 2026) will be summed. If neither Farside nor SoSoValue has published the relevant daily flows by June 19, 2026, the question resolves based on aggregate weekly figures cited by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) for the week of June 8–12, 2026.

factors.description: 15 consecutive trading days of net outflows (May 11 - June 1, 2026), the longest streak in 2026. Strong bearish momentum.
Average daily ETH ETF outflow in May was -$27.1M/day, with recent weeks averaging -$43 to -$60M/day. A 5-day positive week requires overcoming ~$135-300M in expected outflows.
Only 5 of 20 trading days in May 2026 were positive (25%), showing isolated inflow days are rare and insufficient to offset outflow days.
ETH price at ~$1,987, down 59.8% from ATH. Depressed prices reduce institutional appetite and attract more outflows from loss-cutting.
BlackRock ETHA drove 77% of outflows, suggesting large institutional repositioning that may run its course, potentially allowing reversal.
Extended outflow streaks historically revert - contrarian signal noted by analysts. Week of May 1-8 saw +$171.5M, proving positive weeks are possible even in bearish months.
Both BTC and ETH ETFs seeing severe outflows suggests macro-driven selling. If macro conditions improve (rate cut signals, risk-on shift), both could reverse together.

input.anchor: Historical base rate: In May 2026, 1 of 4 weeks (25%) had net positive ETH ETF flows. However, adjusting for the current 15-day outflow streak and accelerating outflow magnitude, the conditional probability is lower, roughly 15-20%.
input.assessment: The ETH ETF flow data shows an extremely bearish trend: 15 consecutive outflow days, accelerating weekly outflows (-$255M, -$216M, -$241.6M), and only 25% of May trading days positive. The question asks about the week of June 8-12, which is 4-5 trading days after the question close. For YES resolution, the sum of 5 days must be strictly positive. Given the current trajectory of -$27 to -$60M per day on average, this requires a dramatic reversal. However, extended outflow streaks can reverse (May 1-8 was +$171.5M after prior weakness), and concentrated ETHA outflows may exhaust themselves. The probability is low but not negligible - approximately 15-20%.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor of ~20% based on May weekly data. Strong negative factors push this lower. Small contrarian/reversion factor partially offsets. Final estimate of 18% feels appropriate - not too low (streaks do end) but reflects strong bearish momentum.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the BlackRock ETHA institutional repositioning has completed; whether any macro catalyst (Fed signals, regulatory news) could trigger a sudden reversal; whether the 6+ days between now and June 8 see any trend change.
input.tool_audit: research() provided excellent Farside Investors data with complete daily ETH ETF flow breakdowns. SoSoValue was blocked by Cloudflare. The second research query on ETH sentiment failed due to tool unavailability but the first query covered price data adequately.
input.process_reflection: The research tool worked well for the primary data source (Farside). The forced StructuredOutput requirement created pressure to rush analysis. The reflection/premortem gate is valuable but adds latency when the system is pushing for immediate output.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
**Evidence verification:** All seven factors are solidly grounded in the research trace (Farside Investors data retrieved via the research tool).

- **Factor 1** (15 consecutive outflow days, May 11-June 1): Trace confirms "15 consecutive outflow sessions running from May 11 through June 1, 2026."
- **Factor 2** (-$27.1M/day average, recent weeks -$43 to -$60M/day): Trace confirms May total -$541.3m / 20 days = -$27.1m/day, and weekly averages of -$51.0, -$43.2, and -$60.4M/day.
- **Factor 3** (5 of 20 positive days = 25%): Trace confirms "Only 5 of 20 trading days in May were positive."
- **Factor 4** (ETH ~$1,987, down 59.8%): Trace confirms from CoinGecko data.
- **Factor 5** (ETHA 77% of outflows): Trace confirms -$418.7m of -$541.3m.
- **Factor 6** (May 1-8 was +$171.5M): Trace confirms.
- **Factor 7** (BTC ETFs also in severe outflows): Trace confirms -$2,406.0m in May.

**Double-counting:** Factors 1, 2, and 3 all describe the same bearish outflow trend from different angles (streak length, magnitude, frequency of positive days). The agent explicitly recognized this in its thinking: "the negative factors are somewhat overlapping/correlated...So there's some double-counting in the factor sum." This is why the agent departed from the factor-implied ~4.2% to settle at 18%. The self-correction is appropriate.

**Wrong-direction factors:** All logit signs are correct.

**Assessment consistency:** The assessment concludes ~18%, consistent with the anchor of 15-20% and the heavily bearish factor set. No contradiction.

**Resolution criteria:** Fully provided and properly engaged. The agent correctly identifies Farside Investors as the primary resolution source and targets the right week (June 8-12, 2026).

**Probability assessment:** My independent estimate is 12-20%. The May base rate of ~25% positive weeks should be adjusted downward for the 15-day streak and accelerating outflows, but upward slightly for mean-reversion potential given the extended streak and 6+ trading days between now and the target week. 18% falls comfortably within my range.

**Anchor divergence:** Anchor is 15-20%, final estimate is 18%. No meaningful departure.

**Counterargument quality:** Strong — constructs a coherent mean-reversion narrative, identifies specific catalysts, notes BlackRock repositioning could exhaust itself, and argues 18% might be too low. This is a genuine alternative story.

**Self-confidence:** 0.45 is appropriately modest for a forecast about financial flows 8+ trading days out with high uncertainty about trend persistence.

**Tool audit:** Agent claims "second research query on ETH sentiment failed due to tool unavailability." Ground truth shows 3 calls, 0 errors. The research call itself succeeded (no error), but the sub-agent's internal web tools were unavailable, producing an essentially empty result. This is a minor imprecision in characterization — the tool call succeeded but produced no useful data. Not materially misleading.

**Cross-question consistency:** The related ETH/BTC ratio forecast (question 43747, 83% YES) used "Persistent ETH ETF outflows (-$541M in May)" as a bearish factor on ETH. This is fully consistent with the current forecast's bearish view on ETH ETF flows. No contradiction.

No errors found that would change the forecast direction or magnitude.
